postmanEvidence: Postman Desktop 11.71.7 packages the visualizer sandbox surface and Handlebars 4.7 line.
Postman Desktop 11.71.7 visualizer behavior is source-audited for the sandboxed iframe and Handlebars package line.
Postman Desktop 11.71.7 packages handlebars@4.7.8 through the visualizer runtime path.
Postman Desktop 11.71.7 visualizer docs/source expose pm.getData(callback) inside the rendered document.
Postman Desktop 11.71.7 visualizer docs/source expose pm.visualizer.set(layout, data, options).

postmeterRuntime.reason: Handlebars 4.7.8 is the audited Postman Desktop 11.71.7 line, but current npm advisories cover <=4.7.8. PostMeter uses the patched 4.7-compatible browser runtime and records the vendored source SHA-256 in src/core/visualizerHandlebarsBundle.js.

coveredBehavior: pm.visualizer.set and pm.visualizer.clear capture bounded isolated render output

Handlebars.compile, precompile, template, create, helpers, block helpers, dynamic partials, inline partial decorator, custom decorators, runtime helper options, SafeString, block params, data variables, parent/root lookup, whitespace behavior, and noEscape compile option
pm.getData(callback) remains available only inside the isolated visualizer document
Reviewed JS/CSS visualizer assets require sha256 integrity and are injected into the isolated visualizer document; unreviewed remote/file/network loads remain stripped by sanitizer and iframe CSP
Unsafe constructor, prototype, and __proto__ data/helper-option keys are removed before render and callback handoff

limitations: This artifact records runtime/source evidence and PostMeter coverage for the Postman Desktop Handlebars line; it is not a license to load unreviewed remote visualizer assets.
PostMeter intentionally uses handlebars@4.7.9 rather than the exact Postman Desktop handlebars@4.7.8 package because the exact package line has active high/critical advisories.
notes: This fixture closes the visualizer.handlebars-full-parity default-import claim blocker while preserving the isolated iframe CSP and no Node/Electron/renderer-access sandbox boundary.
